questions.q: A point charge is placed at a distance $r$ from center of a conducting neutral sphere of radius $R(r\gt$ $R$ ). The potential at any point $P$ inside the sphere at a distance $r_1$ from point charge due to induced charge of the sphere is given by $\left[\mathrm{k}=\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\right]$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A thin semi-circular ring of radius $r$ has a positive charge $q$ distributed uniformly over it. The net field $\vec{E}$ at the centre $O$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Linear charge density } \lambda=\left(\frac{q}{\pi r}\right) \\ & \begin{aligned} E & =\int d E \sin \theta(-\hat{j})=\int \frac{K \cdot d q}{r^2} \sin \theta(-\hat{j}) \\ E & =\frac{K}{r^2} \int \frac{q r}{\pi r} d \theta \sin \theta(-\hat{j}) \\ & =\frac{K}{r^2} \frac{q}{\pi} \int_0^\pi \sin \theta(-\hat{j})=\frac{q}{2 \pi^2 \varepsilon_0 r^2}(-\hat{j})\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $P(r)=\frac{Q}{\pi R^4} r$ be the charge density distribution for a solid sphere of radius $R$ and total charge $Q$ for a point ' $p$ ' inside the sphere at distance $r_1$ from the centre of the sphere, the magnitude of electric field is
questions.solution: $\mathrm{E} 4 \pi \mathrm{r}_1^2=\frac{\int_0^{r_1} \frac{Q}{\pi R^4} r 4 \pi r^2 d r}{\varepsilon_0} \Rightarrow E=\frac{Q r_1^2}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0 R^4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
